sections.paragraph:
ὑπέχω fut. inf. ὑφέξειν (Ath.); 2 aor. ὑπέσχον. Mid.: fut. 3 sg. ὑφέξεται PsSol (Hom.+; ins, pap, LXX [PsSol]) in our lit. only as a legal t.t. δίκην ὑπέχειν undergo punishment (Soph. et al. [δίκη 1]; PHal 1, 163 [III b.c.]; PFay 21, 25 [II a.d.] ὅπως τὴν προσήκουσαν δίκην ὑπόσχωσι; Mitt-Wilck. I/2, 469, 10; Jos., C. Ap. 2, 194, Ant. 1, 99; Ath. 3, 1) Jd 7. Also ὑπ. τιμωρίαν MPol 6:2 (Ath. 2, 1; cp. 2 Macc 4:48 ζημίαν ὑπ.; Theophyl. Sim., Ep. 68 ὑπ. κόλασιν).—M-M.

fut. fut. = fut.

inf. inf. = infinitive

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PHal PHal = Dikaiomata: in a papyrus published by the Graeca Halensis—List 4

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

Mitt-Wilck. Mitt-Wilck. = LMitteis/UWilcken, Grundzüge und Chrestomathie der Papyruskunde—Lists 4, 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1035
